question: [File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/db/db.go -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: GetDbWithContext()] Can an unprivileged external user send requests that cause the statementTimeout (default 20 seconds) to be reached for every query, generating a flood of context.DeadlineExceeded errors that overwhelm the error handling path and cause the Rosetta node's goroutine pool to grow unboundedly, crashing ≥30% of nodes?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/db/db.go -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: ConnectToDb()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the DSN format in GetDsn() (host=%s port=%d user=%s dbname=%s password=%s sslmode=disable) by observing network traffic (since TLS is disabled), extracting DB credentials, and using them to open direct connections to PostgreSQL that exhaust the connection limit and deny service to ≥30% of Rosetta nodes?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/db/db.go -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: ConnectToDb()] Can an unprivileged external user trigger a slow-read attack on the Rosetta HTTP API, causing request handlers to hold DB connections (acquired via GetDbWithContext()) for extended periods, exhausting the MaxOpenConnections pool and causing ≥30% of Rosetta nodes to queue all new requests indefinitely?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/db/db.go -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: NewDbClient()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit a race condition where NewDbClient() stores a reference to the gorm.DB instance that is concurrently being closed or reset, causing concurrent requests to use a nil or invalid DB handle and panic, crashing ≥30% of Rosetta nodes?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/db/db.go -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: ConnectToDb()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that ConnectToDb() does not set SetConnMaxIdleTime(), causing idle connections to never expire regardless of inactivity, holding PostgreSQL backend processes indefinitely and reducing available connections for ≥30% of Rosetta nodes under load?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/db/db.go -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: GetDbWithContext()] Can an unprivileged external user send requests that trigger the context.WithTimeout() path in GetDbWithContext() with a very large statementTimeout value, causing queries to run for up to that duration and hold connections, allowing the user to exhaust the connection pool with just a few concurrent requests and degrade ≥30% of Rosetta nodes?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/db/db.go -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: ConnectToDb()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the sequential nature of ConnectToDb() (gorm.Open then db.DB() then pool config) to cause a time-of-check-to-time-of-use (TOCTOU) race where the DB connection is valid at open time but becomes invalid before pool settings are applied, causing SetMaxOpenConns() to operate on a broken pool and crash ≥30% of nodes?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/db/db.go -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: ConnectToDb()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the gormlogrus logger (gormlogrus.New()) configured in ConnectToDb() to cause log injection by crafting requests that generate SQL queries with newline characters in parameters, polluting log output and potentially causing log-based monitoring to miss critical errors, degrading ≥30% of nodes' observability and causing undetected failures?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/db/db.go -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: ConnectToDb()] Can an unprivileged external user send requests that cause the Rosetta node to repeatedly call ConnectToDb() on reconnection attempts (if the node has reconnect logic), each call creating a new connection pool without closing the old one, causing connection leaks that exhaust PostgreSQL's max_connections and degrade ≥30% of nodes?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/db/db.go -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: GetDbWithContext()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that GetDbWithContext() creates a new context.WithTimeout() for every request without any rate limiting, by sending thousands of concurrent requests that each create a goroutine blocked on DB I/O, exhausting the Go runtime's goroutine scheduler and causing ≥30% of Rosetta nodes to become unresponsive?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/db/db.go -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: ConnectToDb()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the unencrypted DB connection (sslmode=disable) to inject a TCP RST packet that terminates the PostgreSQL connection mid-query, causing gorm to return an error that is not properly handled, leaving the connection in an inconsistent state in the pool and causing ≥30% of subsequent requests to fail?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/db/db.go -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: ConnectToDb()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the pool's MaxLifetime of 30 minutes (default) by sending requests that keep connections active just under the lifetime limit, preventing connection recycling and causing the pool to hold stale connections that fail when PostgreSQL restarts, crashing ≥30% of Rosetta nodes simultaneously?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/db/db.go -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: GetDbWithContext()] Can an unprivileged external user send requests where the parent context is cancelled immediately after GetDbWithContext() returns but before the query executes, causing the DB query to be cancelled and the connection to be returned to the pool in an unknown state, leading to pool corruption that degrades ≥30% of Rosetta nodes?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/db/db.go -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: ConnectToDb()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that ConnectToDb() does not validate dbConfig.Pool values before calling SetMaxIdleConns(), SetConnMaxLifetime(), and SetMaxOpenConns(), allowing a zero or negative MaxOpenConnections to be set (meaning unlimited), enabling the user to open unlimited connections through the API and crash ≥30% of nodes?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/db/db.go -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: ConnectToDb()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of circuit breaker logic in ConnectToDb() to cause a cascade failure where one Rosetta node's DB connection failure causes it to return nil (line 28), and callers that don't check for nil propagate panics that crash ≥30% of dependent nodes?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/db/db.go -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: GetDbWithContext()] Can an unprivileged external user send requests that cause GetDbWithContext() to be called with an already-expired context, causing every DB query to immediately fail with context.DeadlineExceeded, and by doing this at scale across multiple Rosetta nodes, cause ≥30% of nodes to report unhealthy and be removed from the network?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/db/db.go -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: ConnectToDb()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the DB pool is shared across all request handlers in a Rosetta node, by sending a mix of fast and slow requests that cause slow requests to hold connections while fast requests queue, creating a head-of-line blocking scenario that makes ≥30% of nodes appear unresponsive?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/db/db.go -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: ConnectToDb()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the lack of TLS (sslmode=disable in GetDsn()) to perform ARP spoofing on the local network, redirecting DB traffic to a rogue server that returns malformed PostgreSQL protocol responses, causing gorm to panic and crash ≥30% of Rosetta nodes?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/db/db.go -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: ConnectToDb()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that SetMaxIdleConns() is set to 20 (default) while MaxOpenConnections is 100, causing 80 connections to be closed after each request burst, generating excessive TCP connection churn that overwhelms the OS's TIME_WAIT state and degrades ≥30% of Rosetta nodes' network performance?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/db/db.go -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: GetDbWithContext()] Can an unprivileged external user send requests that trigger the statementTimeout path in GetDbWithContext(), causing context.WithTimeout() to create a timer for each request, and by sending thousands of concurrent requests, cause timer heap exhaustion in the Go runtime that degrades ≥30% of Rosetta nodes?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/db/db.go -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: ConnectToDb()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that ConnectToDb() uses postgres.Open() which does not validate the DSN until the first query, causing the node to start successfully but fail on the first request, and if this happens simultaneously across multiple nodes (e.g., after a config change), cause ≥30% of nodes to fail simultaneously?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/db/db.go -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: ConnectToDb()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the connection pool's lack of health checking (no periodic Ping()) to cause connections that have been silently dropped by a firewall or NAT timeout to remain in the idle pool, causing the next request to fail with a broken pipe error and degrade ≥30% of Rosetta nodes?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/db/db.go -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: GetDbWithContext()] Can an unprivileged external user send requests that cause GetDbWithContext() to return a DB handle with a context that expires during a multi-step transaction, causing the transaction to be rolled back mid-way and leaving the DB in an inconsistent state that causes subsequent queries to fail, degrading ≥30% of Rosetta nodes?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/db/db.go -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: ConnectToDb()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that gorm.Open() with postgres driver establishes a lazy connection (not immediately validated), causing ConnectToDb() to return a seemingly valid DbClient that fails on first use, and if multiple Rosetta nodes start simultaneously with a DB issue, cause ≥30% to fail at the same time?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/db/db.go -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: ConnectToDb()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the shared PostgreSQL instance (all Rosetta nodes connect to the same DB) by sending requests that trigger table-level locks or long-running transactions, causing other nodes' queries to queue and time out, effectively shutting down ≥30% of nodes' ability to process requests?
